title: A Leader Steps Down at a Famous Arts Center
summary: Ric Grenell is leaving his job as president of the Kennedy Center, a big performing arts place in Washington, D.C. President Trump announced this on social media. Grenell helped make changes at the center, like adding Trump's name to it. Many artists, such as those from the musical "Hamilton," stopped performing there because of these changes. The Kennedy Center is known for honoring famous artists and bringing people together, but it became more connected to Trump's ideas. Now, Matt Floca will take over as president. The center will close soon for a two-year renovation to make it better. Grenell has worked for Trump in other jobs, like being an ambassador.

background_read: The Kennedy Center is a famous arts venue in Washington, D.C., where people watch performances like plays and concerts. It gives awards to artists every year. Recently, it has been in the news because President Trump and his team made changes, like adding his name to the building. This upset many artists who perform there. Now, the center is getting a new leader and will close for repairs to make it better for future shows.
Article_Structure: WHO: Ric Grenell, President Trump, Matt Floca, and artists like those from "Hamilton"
WHAT: Grenell is stepping down as president of the Kennedy Center, and Floca will replace him; changes at the center led artists to leave
WHERE: At the Kennedy Center in Washington, D.C.
WHY: Because of changes made by Trump and Grenell, and the center is closing for renovation

perspectives.description: Some people might think adding Trump's name and making changes is good to update the center.
Many artists are unhappy and left because they feel the changes are too political and not about art.
